Empires & Puzzles Hilda Review: The Fearless Gargoyle

Don't let the adorable spots fool you—Hilda is a menace. We review this Holy Gargoyle to see if her unique blend of bleed damage, healing theft, and buff blocking can still compete with the titans of the 2026 meta. Is her Softskin enough to keep her relevant?
Hilda Review: Cute, Fluffy, and Surprisingly Deadly
Hilda: The "Cutesy" Nightmare of the Sanctuary
Meet Hilda, the Gargoyle family's attempt at rebranding from "stone-cold killers" to "plushie merchandise." Released back in June 2024 as part of the

event, she stands out as a 5-star Holy (Yellow) Fighter who combines heavy hitting with a deceptively supportive kit. Unlike her stony brethren who look like they belong on a gothic cathedral, Hilda looks like she belongs in a toddler's crib. But make no mistake—this furball bites.

Let's address the elephant—or rather, the spotted winged gerbil—in the room. Hilda is adorable. With her bright yellow fur, purple spots, and a little red bow, she is the antithesis of the gritty, dark aesthetic usually found in Empires & Puzzles. It’s a hilarious contrast when you see her terrifying stone skin activate, reminding you that beneath that cuddly exterior lies a monster capable of tanking a nuclear blast.
Let's be real—time has not been kind to Hilda's raw numbers. With a base power of
, she is significantly behind the modern curve.
Compared to modern hitters pushing 1300+, she hits softly on paper.
These were tank-worthy stats in 2024. Today? They are merely "average." New heroes are boasting 2200+ HP out of the box.
passive to survive. Her raw stats alone won't keep her alive against the modern gods of E&P.
"Drain Strike" is a Swiss Army knife of misery for your opponents.
435% to three targets is a solid multiplier. Even with her aging attack stat, this can still cripple a flank.
This is her signature move. She doesn't just damage you; she effectively vampirizes the bleed damage for her whole team as
. It turns her into a pseudo-healer who punishes the enemy for existing.

Let's crunch the numbers to see if she still holds up.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Hilda might have old stats, but her passive game is timeless.
Immunity to Poison and Burn is excellent situational utility, especially against DOT-heavy teams. It's a nice bonus, but not the main event.
is the reason you play this hero.
(technically -90%, but often single digits) for the next
Immunity to status ailments and mana reduction during this time.
In a world of crazy snipers and one-shot wonders, Softskin forces the enemy to waste resources "peeling" her skin before they can hurt her. It makes her deceptively tanky despite her lower HP pool.
fits her perfectly. It adds another layer of survival on top of Softskin and Fighter revive. If they don't miss her, they hit her for 1 damage. If they kill her, she revives. It's infuriating.
Upon release, Hilda was hailed as a "beast" and a "must-have" for her ability to shut down buffs and sustain the team. She was a staple in War defenses and a terror in Rush wars.
The damage creep has been real. Modern heroes hit harder, faster, and have nearly double her effective health pool. Her 435% hit doesn't scare a team with 3500 HP heroes as much as it used to.
She is no longer a top-tier main defender. However, she is still a
. Her specific combination of Buff Immunity and Softskin makes her an excellent counter to specific meta threats. In

tournaments, she is arguably S-Tier because she provides "healing" via Boosted Health (which is often allowed or bypasses certain restrictions depending on the specific rule interpretation, though usually Bleed steal counts as healing and is blocked—but the damage and buff block remain elite).
In Bloody Battle, the healing is blocked, but the Buff Immunity and raw damage are king.
Hilda is a Fighter, and her role is to deal damage to generate big bleeds.
Maximizes the initial hit (435% is a high multiplier) and increases the Bleed damage (which scales with stats). More bleed means more healing.
Lowers her HP pool, making her more reliant on Softskin.
Increases survivability significantly. Since she has Softskin, she effectively has "more" HP than the numbers show.
Her bleed damage softens, reducing the team's passive healing.
. Hilda needs to hurt people to be relevant. Her Softskin and Fighter revive are her survival mechanisms; she doesn't need to be a meat shield.
Softskin is one of the best defensive passives in the game.
Buff Immunity for 4 turns is a long time in E&P.
Turning enemy pain (bleed) into ally gain (boosted health).
Fighter is the best class for defense. Revive + Softskin = Rage quit.
Her stats are undeniably dated compared to late-2025/2026 heroes.
She has no cleanse or dispel herself.
Average speed is "slow" in the current Very Fast meta.
Heroes immune to bleed (like other Gargoyles or Construct heroes) negate her healing mechanic.
Excellent for dismantling teams that rely on buffs.
At Very Fast speed, she is a nightmare. Keeping Softskin up 100% of the time makes her nearly invincible.
She shuts down the enemy's ability to get buffs.
Softskin allows her to cheese through hard hitting bosses.
Synergy: The "Death by a Thousand Cuts"
Pair her with other Bleed or DOT heroes. While DOTs overwrite each other if they are the
type (Bleed overwrites Bleed), pairing her with Burn or Poison heroes creates overwhelming pressure.
Be careful not to overwrite her Bleed with a weaker Bleed! Her bleed heals the team; standard bleeds do not.
Protect Hilda while she charges. Once she fires, she protects herself with Softskin and protects the team by healing them.
A simple cleanser removes her Bleed and Buff Immunity. (e.g., Rigard, or newer meta cleansers).
Heroes who block ailments (like newer Construct or Astral heroes) make her useless.
Enemy Gargoyles are immune to Poison/Burn, but not Bleed. However, many modern families have innate resistances.
If you pulled Hilda from the portal or Soul Exchange:
Even with "older" stats, her mechanics are god-tier for a developing roster. She will carry you through map stages (Softskin tanks bosses) and win you raids you shouldn't win.

She is a luxury tool. Do not prioritize her over the latest 1300+ Power powerhouse unless you specifically need a Yellow Buff Blocker for War depth. She is worthy of emblems, but think twice before giving her your precious Alpha Aethers unless you use her daily.
On release, the forum was split between "OMG Adorable" and "Why is this Pokemon in my dark fantasy game?" One user noted,
"She looks like she belongs in a toddler's crib, not a battlefield."
Even back in 2024, players noted the gap.
"Her release this month really highlights just how far behind the current HOTM have fallen,"
one player lamented, comparing her to Aviana.
Her kit was so loaded that players jokingly (or seriously) suggested she should be classified as a "6-star" compared to the weaker heroes of her time.

Hilda is the classic example of "Mechanics over Stats." While her numbers may look dusty in 2026, the ability to take 1 damage from a sniper shot and prevent the enemy from buffing for 4 turns is timeless. She is a wolf in sheep's clothing—or rather, a stone monster in a plushie's clothing. If you have her, use her. She might not be the queen of the arena anymore, but she’s certainly the princess of annoyance.
Still a solid A- tier hero for War depth and Rush formats, but keep her away from the absolute top-tier defenses.
A detailed review and strategy guide for Hilda, the 5-star Holy Gargoyle hero. We analyze her stats, special skill, softskin passive, and viability in the current 2026 meta.
